title: Rivian-Volkswagen Joint Venture Deal Details
text: Rivian Automotive and Volkswagen Group announced a joint venture worth up to $5.8 billion, representing an increase from the initially announced $5 billion deal. At closing of the Joint Venture, Volkswagen Group invested about $1.3 billion as consideration for background IP licenses and a 50% equity stake in the joint venture. The remaining investment of up to $3.5 billion is expected to come in the form of equity, a convertible note, and debt at future dates and based on clearly defined milestones. The cash Volkswagen will invest under the deal makes it more likely that Rivian will survive long enough to get to sustainable scale. This is critical given that Rivian was losing somewhere around $1.5 billion per quarter. The investment will provide Rivian the funding necessary to develop its less expensive and smaller R2 SUVs set to roll out in early 2026 and its planned R3 crossovers. The partnership will enable Rivian to cut operating costs by leveraging volumes of supplies including chips and components. The JV will permit Volkswagen to license Rivian's vehicle and software architectural intellectual property. The integration of Rivian's software is expected to start with the Volkswagen brand, followed by Audi and VW's forthcoming Scout brand, with first VW models arriving as early as 2027. Following the announcement, Rivian's shares surged approximately 50% in extended trading.
